question.text: For an infrastructure project to qualify as a "completed and revenue-generating project" under InvIT Regulations, it must have been generating revenue from operations for a period of not less than:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A "completed and revenue-generating project" means a project that has achieved commercial operations date, received all requisite approvals, and has been generating revenue from operations for a period of not less than one year. **The Trap:** Six months might seem reasonable but the regulation clearly specifies one year. This ensures the project has a demonstrated track record. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The one-year revenue track record requirement ensures that only projects with proven revenue generation capacity qualify, protecting investor interests.
